claims.claim: Opioid Substitution Therapy Waitlist Grows to 8,500 Patients
claims.source: No Malaysian government, regulator, court, or peer-reviewed primary source found for an 8,500 OST waitlist as of December 2025. Searches included MOH, AADK, and exact-number web searches on 2026-05-17.
claims.correction: Remove the 8,500 waitlist figure unless a primary source can be supplied. Current public AADK/MOH material found does not substantiate it.
claims.stale_check: The analysis is dated 2026-05-17 and says December 2025; no later primary source was found that validates the number.

claims.claim: Methadone and buprenorphine treatment programs cannot accommodate growing demand from opioid-dependent patients.
claims.source: MOH Pharmaceutical Services Programme confirms permits for methadone/buprenorphine in Opioid Replacement Therapy, but no primary source found showing unmet demand or inability to accommodate demand: https://pharmacy.moh.gov.my/ms/entri/pengeluaran-permit-bahan-psikotropik-methadone-buprenorphine.html
claims.correction: State only that methadone and/or buprenorphine are regulated for opioid replacement therapy in Malaysia unless a capacity or waitlist source is provided.
claims.stale_check: No evidence found of a recent official capacity report validating this claim.

claims.claim: 8,500 patients on waitlist for opioid substitution therapy as of December 2025
claims.source: No primary source found. AADK's public statistics page lists drug statistics reports, but available public sources found did not contain this waitlist figure: https://www.aadk.gov.my/statistik-dadah/
claims.correction: Do not publish this as fact without an official MOH/AADK dataset, parliamentary answer, or report.
claims.stale_check: The claim is fixed to December 2025; no later official publication found confirming it.

claims.claim: Only 52,000 of estimated 170,000 opioid users receive any form of treatment
claims.source: Malaysian Journal of Medicine article states about 52,000 patients reported receiving OMT in December 2012 and separately cites 170,000 injecting drug users, not 'opioid users' or 'any form of treatment': https://www.e-mjm.org/2015/v70n2/opioid-dependence-treatment.pdf. AADK 2024 report records 39,895 opiate-category drug users among 192,857 total drug/substance users in 2024: https://www.aadk.gov.my/wp-content/uploads/BMD2024_web.pdf
claims.correction: Do not combine the 2012 OMT figure with an older 170,000 injecting-drug-user estimate as if it were a December 2025 treatment coverage denominator. Use current AADK/MOH figures and distinguish OMT from all treatment/rehabilitation.
claims.stale_check: The 52,000 figure is from December 2012, so it is stale for a 2025/2026 analysis.

claims.claim: Average wait time for methadone program entry is 4.5 months in Klang Valley
claims.source: No primary Malaysian source found for a 4.5-month Klang Valley methadone-entry wait time. Searches found unrelated study snippets about 4-month follow-up/entry but not this statistic.
claims.correction: Remove or qualify this claim unless backed by an MOH/AADK clinic-access dataset, parliamentary reply, or peer-reviewed Klang Valley study.
claims.stale_check: No recent official source found validating the figure.

claims.claim: Patients waiting for treatment face overdose risk 6 times higher than those in treatment
claims.source: BMJ peer-reviewed systematic review found mortality rates are higher out of opioid substitution treatment than in treatment, but the available text does not verify a precise '6 times' overdose-risk ratio for people on waitlists: Sordo et al., BMJ 2017, https://www.bmj.com/content/357/bmj.j1550
claims.correction: Say OST is associated with substantially lower all-cause and overdose mortality, and cite the exact rate ratio from the specific study used. Do not apply it specifically to 'waitlist' patients unless the study population matches.
claims.stale_check: The evidence is older but still relevant; no newer Malaysia-specific primary source was found.

claims.claim: Government methadone clinics operate only during office hours excluding working patients
claims.source: Selangor Health Department official methadone clinic listing shows at least one clinic with Saturday, Sunday and public-holiday methadone hours: https://www.jknselangor.moh.gov.my/index.php/bahagian/khidmat-kesihatan/klinik-methadone. Kuala Lumpur/Putrajaya official clinic pages show some clinics operate 8am-5pm weekdays and list methadone services: https://jknkl.moh.gov.my/jkwpklp/ms/klinik-kesihatan-kuala-lumpur
claims.correction: Replace the blanket claim with a facility-specific statement. Some government clinics appear to operate on weekday office hours, but at least one official listing shows weekend/public-holiday methadone service.
claims.stale_check: Sources crawled/updated in 2025-2026, so this is current enough to contradict the blanket claim.

claims.claim: Rigid scheduling forces patients to choose between treatment and employment
claims.source: No primary Malaysian patient-outcome, labour, or clinic-access source found. Some official clinic schedules are limited, but this causal employment claim was not independently verified.
claims.correction: Frame as a hypothesis or patient-reported barrier only if supported by interviews, survey data, or official access research.

claims.claim: France expanded OST through community pharmacies reaching 180,000 patients since 2018
claims.source: OFDT/EMCDDA treatment workbook says France had approximately 180,000 OST clients in 2019 and notes delivery through community pharmacies; it does not say the expansion began 'since 2018': https://en.ofdt.fr/sites/ofdt/files/2025-01/france2024_05_en_treatment_vf.pdf. OFDT also reports 177,000 in 2019: https://www.ofdt.fr/publication/2023/traitements-de-substitution-aux-opioides-en-france-bilan-2023-519
claims.correction: Say France had roughly 177,000-180,000 OST recipients around 2019, with substantial community-pharmacy dispensing. Remove 'since 2018' unless a specific 2018 reform is cited.
claims.stale_check: Newer reporting estimates about 171,000 in 2024, so 180,000 is not the latest figure.

claims.claim: Pharmacy-based distribution dramatically increased treatment accessibility and retention
claims.source: PLOS One peer-reviewed article states the French buprenorphine model, where prescriptions can be delivered by a patient-named pharmacy, rapidly proved efficient for public health, access to care and risk reduction: https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0137708. The source found does not substantiate the word 'dramatically' or a retention effect.
claims.correction: Use narrower wording: French pharmacy dispensing is associated with broad access to care and risk reduction; provide separate evidence if claiming improved retention.
claims.stale_check: The model is longstanding; newer OFDT data show continued large-scale use but not necessarily a recent dramatic increase.

claims.claim: Making people wait months for addiction treatment is sentencing them to continued suffering
claims.source: Normative claim built on the unverified Malaysia wait-time claim. WHO/UNODC standards support accessible pharmacological treatment for opioid dependence but do not verify Malaysia-specific months-long waits: https://www.unodc.org/documents/drug-prevention-and-treatment/UNODC-WHO_International_Standards_Treatment_Drug_Use_Disorders_April_2020.pdf
claims.correction: Retain only as opinion after clearly separating it from unverified waitlist/wait-time facts.

claims.claim: Like making diabetics queue 8 months for insulin
claims.source: No primary source found for an 8-month OST wait, and the earlier card claimed 4.5 months rather than 8 months.
claims.correction: Remove the 8-month figure or label it clearly as analogy rather than fact; it conflicts with the card's own 4.5-month assertion.

claims.claim: Addiction is a medical condition with effective treatment
claims.source: UNODC/WHO International Standards for the Treatment of Drug Use Disorders, 2020, state pharmacological treatment options should include methadone and buprenorphine for opioid agonist maintenance and opioid withdrawal: https://www.unodc.org/documents/drug-prevention-and-treatment/UNODC-WHO_International_Standards_Treatment_Drug_Use_Disorders_April_2020.pdf. MOH Malaysia describes methadone replacement therapy as a harm-reduction programme for opioid abusers: https://pharmacy.moh.gov.my/en/content/methadone-replacement-therapy.html

claims.claim: The waitlist converts a treatable condition into preventable suffering
claims.source: The underlying waitlist and wait-time figures are unverified. General treatment effectiveness is supported by UNODC/WHO, but Malaysia-specific waitlist harm is not verified: https://www.unodc.org/documents/drug-prevention-and-treatment/UNODC-WHO_International_Standards_Treatment_Drug_Use_Disorders_April_2020.pdf
claims.correction: Keep as commentary only after removing or sourcing the specific waitlist evidence.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: 8,500 patients on waitlist for opioid substitution therapy as of December 2025
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Specific number and date could not be traced to a primary source.
cardinal_sin_flags.primary_source: No primary source found; AADK statistics page: https://www.aadk.gov.my/statistik-dadah/

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Only 52,000 of estimated 170,000 opioid users receive any form of treatment
cardinal_sin_flags.why: It appears to combine a December 2012 OMT figure with an older injecting-drug-user estimate and recasts it as current 'any form of treatment' coverage.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Average wait time for methadone program entry is 4.5 months in Klang Valley
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Specific local wait-time statistic could not be verified from a primary source.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Patients waiting for treatment face overdose risk 6 times higher than those in treatment
cardinal_sin_flags.why: Evidence supports higher mortality out of treatment, but the exact six-times overdose-risk claim for waitlisted patients was not verified.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Government methadone clinics operate only during office hours excluding working patients
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The blanket 'only during office hours' claim is contradicted by official Selangor listing showing weekend/public-holiday methadone hours for at least one clinic.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: France expanded OST through community pharmacies reaching 180,000 patients since 2018
cardinal_sin_flags.why: France's pharmacy-based OST model predates 2018; 180,000 is an approximate 2017/2019 figure, while newer estimates are closer to 171,000 in 2024.

cardinal_sin_flags.claim: Like making diabetics queue 8 months for insulin
cardinal_sin_flags.why: The analysis gives no source for an 8-month wait and elsewhere claims 4.5 months.

omitted_facts: AADK's 2024 drug information report records 192,857 drug/substance users in 2024, with 39,895 in the opiate category, not 170,000 current opioid users: https://www.aadk.gov.my/wp-content/uploads/BMD2024_web.pdf

Bernama reported AADK's 2025 nationwide treatment/rehabilitation figure as 191,832, down from 192,857 in 2024, but this is all drug abuse treatment/rehabilitation rather than OST-specific coverage: https://www.bernama.com/en/news.php?id=2534579

MOH Pharmaceutical Services Programme confirms methadone replacement therapy is a harm-reduction programme and is supervised by pharmacists after prescription by medical officers: https://pharmacy.moh.gov.my/en/content/methadone-replacement-therapy.html

MOH Pharmaceutical Services Programme confirms registered medical practitioners require permits to purchase/use methadone and/or buprenorphine for opioid replacement therapy: https://pharmacy.moh.gov.my/ms/entri/pengeluaran-permit-bahan-psikotropik-methadone-buprenorphine.html

Official Selangor Health Department clinic listing shows at least one methadone clinic with Saturday, Sunday and public-holiday hours, contradicting the claim that government methadone clinics operate only during office hours: https://www.jknselangor.moh.gov.my/index.php/bahagian/khidmat-kesihatan/klinik-methadone

OFDT estimates France had about 177,000 people receiving opioid substitution medications in 2019 and newer reporting estimates about 171,000 in 2024, making '180,000 since 2018' imprecise and stale: https://www.ofdt.fr/publication/2023/traitements-de-substitution-aux-opioides-en-france-bilan-2023-519
source_assessment: The original analysis cites zero sources. Its strongest claims depend on specific numbers, dates, local wait times, clinic operating hours, and international comparisons, but no primary evidence is attached. The fact-check found relevant government and peer-reviewed sources from MOH Malaysia, AADK, UNODC/WHO, BMJ, and OFDT, but these sources often support only broad background points, not the analysis's precise figures. There is no evident representation of patient voices, clinicians, MOH capacity data, AADK programme data, pharmacy regulators beyond permits, or Malaysian OST outcome studies tied to 2025 conditions.
overall_assessment: The analysis is not reliable for publication in its current form. The core waitlist number, Klang Valley wait time, and 2025 treatment-coverage framing are unverified or misleading; the clinic-hours claim is overbroad and contradicted by official facility information; and the France comparison uses a real policy model but misdates and overstates it. Only the general claim that opioid addiction has effective pharmacological treatment is well supported.
